title: Heart valve support device and methods for making and using the same
abstract: The disclosure relates to a device for supporting functions of a heart valve and methods for making and using the same. The device includes a flow optimizer configured to be located in the valve and having a cross sectional area that reduces a regurgitation orifice of the valve during systole. The device includes an anchoring mechanism coupled to the flow optimizer and configured to fix a position of the flow optimizer relative to the valve. The flow optimizer allows hemodynamic flow during diastole, minimizing risk of inducing atrioventricular pressure gradient and thrombogenesis, and seals or minimizes the regurgitation orifice during systole and reinstates efficacy of the valve. The anchoring system requires no traumatic interaction with the valve, atrium and ventricle. Implantation of the device can be achieved without invasive surgery. The device permits intra-procedural optimization of the positioning of the flow optimizer.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A device for assisting a valve of a heart, comprising: a flow optimizer configured to be located at a position within the valve and having a cross sectional area that reduces a regurgitation orifice of the valve during systole; and an anchoring mechanism coupled to said flow optimizer and configured to fix the position of said flow optimizer relative to the valve. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the cross sectional area during systole is greater than the cross sectional area during diastole. 3. The device of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein said flow optimizer includes: a frame including a plurality of arms each including first and second end regions, the first end regions of the plurality of arms being joined at a common joint, the second end regions extending radially from the common joint; and a covering attached to the plurality of arms and extending between adjacent arms of the plurality of arms, the covering defining the cross sectional area of the flow optimizer. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the covering collapses at least partially in a direction of a hemodynamic flow during diastole. 5. The device of claim 3 or claim 4, wherein the frame has a conical shape, the first end regions of the plurality of arms joining at a central axis of the conical shape. 6. The device of claim 5, wherein the conical shape has a base adjacent to a ventricle of the heart and a vertex adjacent to an atrium of the heart. 7. The device of claim 5 or claim 6, wherein the covering includes a plurality of leaflet layers each arranged concentrically about the central axis. 8. The device of claim 7, wherein the plurality of leaflet layers include two or more leaflet layers, the two or more leaflet layers including first and second leaflet layers that at least partially overlap. 9. The device of claim 8, wherein the first and second leaflet layers open to define a gap for hemodynamic flow during diastole. 10. The device of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the first and second leaflet layer are respectively located proximally and distally from the central axis, an atrium-facing surface of the first leaflet layer overlaps at least partially with a ventricle-facing surface of the second leaflet layer. 11. The device of any one of claims 3-10, wherein the covering inflates at least partially toward native valve leaflets during systole. 12. The device of any one of claims 3-11, wherein the covering at least partially blocks the regurgitation orifice during systole. 13. The device of any one of claims 1-12, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes one or more anchoring arms each including a proximal end region joining at a central axis and a distal end region extending from the central axis, the distal end regions of the anchoring arms being configured to be located at commissures of native valve leaflets. 14. The device of claim 13, wherein the distal end region is configured to mate with an annulus of the valve at the commissures. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein each of the one or more anchoring arms is configured to have a range of shape expansion and adapts to a geometry of the annulus of the valve at the commissures. 16. The device of any one of claims 13-15, wherein the anchoring arms are configured to rotate about the central axis of the anchoring mechanism. 17. The device of claim 16, wherein the one or more anchoring arms rotate about the central axis to match angular distribution of the commissures. 18. The device of claim 16 or claim 17, wherein the one or more anchoring arms include a first anchoring arm, the proximal end region of the first anchoring arm including a cylindrical protrusion aligned with the central axis. 19. The device of claim 18, wherein the one or more anchoring arms include a second anchoring arm, the proximal end region of the second anchoring arm is fixedly connected to an inner core enclosed in the cylindrical protrusion and configured to rotate about the central axis relative to the first cylindrical protrusion. 20. The device of claim 19, wherein rotating the inner core relative to the first cylindrical protrusion changes an angle between the first and second anchoring arms. 21. The device of claim 19 or claim 20, wherein the one or more anchoring arms include a third anchoring arm, the proximal end region of the third anchoring arm including a 

 central portion located between the cylindrical protrusion and the inner core and configured to rotate about the central axis relative to the inner core. 22. The device of any one of claims 19-21, wherein rotating the proximal end region of the third anchoring arm relative to the inner core changes an angle between the second and third anchoring arms. 23. The device of any one of claims 16-22, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes a locking collar configured to fix relative positions among the one or more anchoring arms. 24. The device of any one of claims 16-23, wherein the anchoring arms are configured to rotate prior to loading into a catheter, after deployment in the heart via the catheter, or a combination thereof. 25. The device of any one of claims 13-24, wherein the one or more anchoring arms include three anchoring arms, the distal end region of each of the three anchoring arms being configured to be located at a respective commissure of the native valve leaflets. 26. The device of any one of claims 13-25, wherein each of the one or more anchoring arms includes an intermediate region between the proximal end region and the distal end region, the intermediate region being configured to rest against an inner supra-annular wall of an atrium. 27. The device of any one of claims 13-26, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes a height adjustment mechanism configured to individually control a shape of each of the one or more anchoring arms. 28. The device of claim 27, wherein the height adjustment mechanism includes a cable having a proximal end region slidingly connected to a proximal end region of a first anchoring arm of the one or more anchoring arms, the proximal end region of the first anchoring arm being aligned with the central axis, the cable having a distal end region connected to a distally-extending portion of a selected anchoring arm of the one or more anchoring arms. 29. The device of any one of claims 1-28, further comprising a shaft connecting said flow optimizer and said anchoring mechanism. 30. The device of claim 29, wherein said shaft is threaded such that rotations of said shaft relative to said anchoring mechanism changes a distance between said flow optimizer and said anchoring mechanism. 31. The device of claim 29 or claim 30, wherein said shaft is threaded such that rotations of said shaft relative to said anchoring mechanism changes a radial orientation of said flow optimizer relative to said anchoring mechanism. 32. The device of any one of claims 29-31, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes a locking collar configured to fix a relative position between said flow optimizer and said anchoring mechanism. 33. The device of any one of claims 29-32, wherein said shaft is configured to rotate relative to said anchoring mechanism prior to loading into a catheter, after deployment in the heart via the catheter, or a combination thereof. 34. The device of any one of claims 1-33, wherein each of said flow optimizer and said anchoring mechanism has a crimped conformation adapted to be loaded in a catheter and a deployed conformation upon deployment in the heart. 35. The device of any one of claims 1-34, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes an anchoring device coupled to said flow optimizer and configured to anchor to a vena cava. 36. The device of any one of claims 1-35, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes an atrial anchor coupled to said flow optimizer and configured to anchor to an atrial wall. 37. The device of any one of claims 1-36, wherein said anchoring mechanism includes a ventricular anchor coupled to said flow optimizer and configured to anchor to a ventricular wall. 38. An apparatus for implantation, comprising: an anchoring mechanism including one or more anchoring arms, each arm including a proximal end region joining at a central axis and a distal end region extending from the central axis, the distal end region configured to be respectively located at a commissure of native valve leaflets of a valve of a heart; and an implant connected to said anchoring mechanism and being anchored to the valve via said anchoring mechanism. 39. The apparatus of claim 38, wherein the distal end region is configured to mate with an annulus of the valve at the commissure. 40. A method for deploying a device for supporting functions of a valve of a heart, comprising: delivering a distal end region of a catheter to the valve, the catheter being loaded with the device therein; expanding a flow optimizer of the device in the valve by partially retracting the catheter proximally; and opening an anchoring mechanism of the device by at least partially retracting the catheter proximally, the anchoring mechanism being coupled to the flow optimizer and configured to fix a position of the flow optimizer relative to the valve. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein the anchoring mechanism includes one or more anchoring arms each including a proximal end region joining at a central axis and a distal end region extending from the central axis, wherein said opening includes partially opening the one or more anchoring arms. 42. The method of claim 40 or claim 41, after the partially opening the one or more anchoring arms, further comprising respectively aligning the one or more anchoring arms with commissures of native valve leaflets of the valve.
